evals.prompt: Who did I ghost in the last week? I want to see anyone who messaged me that I haven't replied to.
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts whose last message in a DM conversation was sent by them (not me), within the last 7 days, that I have not responded to. Should include contact name, last message preview, time sent, and platform.
expectations: Queried the messages table filtering by is_from_me = 0 for the last message in each conversation
Filtered to DM conversations only (type = 'dm')
Applied a time window filter on sent_at within the last 7 days
Returned results with contact names and timestamps
Used a window function or subquery to get the last message per conversation
Checked message_reactions to exclude messages the user already reacted to (tapback = acknowledgment)
Used millisecond timestamps correctly (divided by 1000 for display, multiplied unixepoch by 1000 for comparison)

evals.prompt: Who should I follow up with? Show me conversations where I sent the last message and never got a reply.
evals.expected_output: A list of DM conversations where the user sent the most recent message and the other person hasn't responded. Should include the recipient name, what the user said, when it was sent, and the platform.
expectations: Queried messages table filtering by is_from_me = 1 for the last message
Filtered to DM conversations (type = 'dm')
Applied a reasonable time window (at least 3 days since last message)
Returned recipient names, last message content, and sent timestamps
Results are ordered by recency or days waiting
Checked message_reactions to see if the other person reacted (acknowledged but didn't reply)
Used millisecond timestamps correctly

evals.prompt: Who haven't I reached out to in a while that I used to talk to regularly?
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts with significant past message history but no recent communication. Should show contact name, total message count, last message date, and days since last contact.
expectations: Joined contacts with messages through conversation_participants or directly
Compared historical message volume against recency of last message
Applied a minimum message threshold to identify 'regular' contacts
Calculated days since last message
Excluded group-only conversations or handled them separately

evals.prompt: Who in my network might be helpful for fundraising? I'm raising a seed round and want to know who I've talked to about investing, fundraising, or venture capital.
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts who have discussed fundraising/investing topics in messages, with context about the conversations. Should show contact name, company, number of relevant messages, and platforms.
expectations: Used messages_fts full-text search with terms like 'fundraising OR investing OR venture OR seed'
Joined FTS results back to messages and contacts for metadata
Filtered to messages not from the user (is_from_me = 0) to find knowledgeable contacts
Grouped results by contact with mention counts
Returned contact names and companies

evals.prompt: Who should I introduce to each other? Find people in my network who talk about similar things but don't know each other.
evals.expected_output: Pairs of contacts who discuss overlapping topics but don't share any group conversations. Should include both names, companies, and the shared topic area.
expectations: Used FTS search or topic analysis to identify contacts with shared interests
Checked that contact pairs don't share existing conversations
Returned pairs with names and companies
Provided reasoning or topic context for each introduction suggestion

evals.prompt: Catch me up on my conversation with Sarah. What have we been talking about recently?
evals.expected_output: A summary of recent messages with a contact named Sarah, including message content, timestamps, and which platform the conversation is on. Should resolve the contact first.
expectations: Searched contacts table for name matching 'Sarah'
Found conversations via conversation_participants
Retrieved recent messages ordered by sent_at DESC
Included both sent and received messages
Showed platform and timestamp for each message

evals.prompt: Show me all my conversations with James across every platform - iMessage, Slack, WhatsApp, everything.
evals.expected_output: A cross-platform view of all conversations with a contact named James, showing conversation details per platform including last message time and message counts.
expectations: Resolved the contact by searching contacts for 'James'
Joined conversation_participants to conversations
Grouped or listed results by platform
Showed last message timestamps per conversation
Covered multiple platforms if the contact exists on more than one

evals.prompt: What are my most important unread messages right now?
evals.expected_output: A prioritized list of conversations with unread messages, ranked by importance signals like relationship strength and message volume.
expectations: Queried conversations where unread_count > 0
Filtered to conversations where the last message was NOT sent by the user (is_from_me = 0)
Ordered by some importance signal (message frequency, recency, or DM vs group)
Returned conversation names, platforms, unread counts, and last message previews
Distinguished between DM and group conversations, prioritizing DMs

evals.prompt: Which investors have I spoken to in the last month that I haven't followed up with? I had a bunch of intro calls and I want to make sure I'm not dropping the ball.
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts related to investing/fundraising where the conversation has gone cold after initial engagement. Should combine topic search with follow-up detection.
expectations: Used FTS to search for investment/fundraising-related terms
Applied a time window of the last 30 days
Identified conversations where follow-up is needed (last message is from them, or stale)
Returned contact names with conversation context
Combined topic search with recency analysis

evals.prompt: Which prospects went quiet after I sent them pricing info? I want to see deals where I shared pricing or a proposal and then heard nothing back.
evals.expected_output: Conversations where the user discussed pricing/proposals and the other person stopped responding. Should use topic search combined with ghosting pattern.
expectations: Used FTS to search for pricing/proposal-related terms
Identified conversations where the user sent the last substantive message
Applied a staleness threshold for 'went quiet'
Returned contact/conversation names with last message context

evals.prompt: Which candidates haven't responded to my last message in over 5 days? I'm recruiting for a few roles and need to know who's gone dark.
evals.expected_output: A list of conversations where candidates haven't replied within 5 days. Should identify recruiting-related conversations.
expectations: Identified conversations likely related to recruiting (via FTS or context)
Found conversations where is_from_me = 1 on the last message
Filtered by sent_at older than 5 days ago
Returned candidate names, last message content, and days since sent

evals.prompt: Which portfolio founders haven't I checked in with in over a month? I want to make sure I'm staying engaged with all my investments.
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts related to portfolio/investment context with dormant communication patterns (>30 days since last message).
expectations: Applied dormant relationship detection with a 30+ day threshold
Used FTS or contact metadata to identify portfolio-related contacts
Returned contact names with last message dates and days since contact
Ordered by longest gap first

evals.prompt: What have I discussed with the design team this sprint? I need to prep for standup and want to review our recent conversations.
evals.expected_output: A summary of recent messages in conversations involving design team members, filtered to the last 1-2 weeks.
expectations: Searched for conversations or messages related to 'design'
Applied a recent time window (1-2 weeks)
Returned message content with sender names and timestamps
Covered both DM and group conversations

evals.prompt: Which close friends haven't I texted in over 2 weeks? Just looking at iMessage and WhatsApp, not work stuff.
evals.expected_output: A list of contacts from personal messaging platforms (iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal) with no recent messages in the last 2 weeks.
expectations: Filtered to personal platforms (imessage, whatsapp, signal)
Applied dormant relationship detection (>14 days)
Required a minimum message history to qualify as 'close friends'
Excluded work platforms (slack, linkedin)
Returned contact names with last message dates

evals.prompt: Summarize my Slack conversations from today. What's been going on?
evals.expected_output: A summary of today's Slack messages across conversations, showing key discussions and participants.
expectations: Filtered messages to platform = 'slack'
Applied time filter for today (sent_at > start of today)
Grouped by conversation or topic
Included sender names and message content
Covered both DM and channel conversations

evals.prompt: I think I have a bunch of duplicate contacts across platforms. Can you find contacts that exist on multiple platforms but aren't merged? Like someone who's on both iMessage and Slack as separate contacts.
evals.expected_output: A list of contact pairs that likely represent the same person across different platforms, identified by matching handles (phone, email) or identical names.
expectations: Searched contact_handles for matching normalized_value across different contact_ids
Joined to contacts to show names and to contact_sources for platform info
Identified cross-platform duplicates (e.g., same person on iMessage and Slack)
Suggested using cued merge contact CLI command to merge duplicates
Handled phone-number-as-name contacts (name LIKE '+%') as likely duplicates

evals.prompt: I have a contact showing up as just a phone number +13475561329. Can you figure out who this is and if I have them under a real name somewhere?
evals.expected_output: Resolution of the phone number to a named contact by matching handles across the contacts database, with suggestion to merge if a match is found.
expectations: Searched contact_handles for the phone number's normalized_value
Found both the phone-number contact and any named contacts sharing the same number
Showed the named contact's details (name, platform, handles)
Suggested merging the two contacts using cued merge contact
Checked recent messages to provide additional context about who this person is

evals.prompt: Can you look up Arielle Rothman for me? I want to know everything we have locally and anything you can find about her online.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive profile combining local database information (contact handles, message history, platforms) with web-sourced enrichment (LinkedIn, company, role).
expectations: Searched contacts table for name match
Retrieved all contact_handles (email, phone, LinkedIn, etc.)
Checked contact_sources for profile URLs
Reviewed recent messages for contextual clues about role/company
Clearly separated local evidence from web search results

evals.prompt: I reacted to someone's message with a heart but never actually texted them back. Does that count as ghosting? Show me conversations where I only reacted but didn't reply.
evals.expected_output: A list of conversations where the user's only acknowledgment was a reaction (tapback) without a text reply, treating this as a softer form of ghosting.
expectations: Queried message_reactions to find messages the user reacted to
Cross-referenced with messages to check if the user sent a follow-up text after reacting
Filtered to DM conversations
Returned contact names, the reacted message, and time elapsed
Distinguished between reaction-only acknowledgment and full text replies
